error_message: Scan tab shows no inventory — tires/products never load, stuck on 'טוען...'
root_cause: ScanPage rendered <ScanClient /> without AppShell wrapper. useProfile() returns null because ProfileProvider lives inside AppShell. The useEffect guard `if (!profile) return` prevented load() from ever running, leaving items=[] and loading=true indefinitely.

error_message: בהצעות מחיר לא ניתן לבחור רוחב צמיג 125
root_cause: WIDTHS array started at 145, missing 125 and 135
fix: Added 125 and 135 to the beginning of the WIDTHS array

error_message: ייצוא אקסל של מוצרים לא כולל עמודת ברקוד, וייבוא לא מקלוט ברקוד
root_cause: exportExcel() headers and rows did not include barcode. importExcel() did not map barcode column and had no column slot for it.
fix: Added 'ברקוד' as second column in export headers+rows. Updated import to read r[1] as barcode and shifted all subsequent column indices by 1 (name r[1]→r[2], category r[2]→r[3], etc.)

error_message: בחיפוש מוצרים לפי מספר ברקוד לא מוצא את הפריט
root_cause: 1) ProductsClient: hay variable in filter didn't include p.barcode. 2) yard/search route: products OR filter searched name+sku but not barcode column.
fix: 1) Added p.barcode to the hay join in ProductsClient.tsx line 172. 2) Added barcode to select and barcode.ilike to the OR filter in yard/search/route.ts.

error_message: מעקב ספקים (SupplierTrackingClient) — כל הרשומות מוצגות תחת 'ללא ספק' למרות שיש להן supplier_id תקין ובעמוד ספקים (SuppliersClient) השם כן מוצג נכון
root_cause: loadAll() queries suppliers with an explicit column list that includes the new `opening_balance` column added in supabase/migrations/066_supplier_opening_balance.sql. If that migration hasn't actually been applied to the live DB yet, the query errors (column does not exist) and `suppRes.data` is null, so `if (suppRes.data) setSuppliers(...)` silently skips — leaving `suppliers` state as an empty array for the whole session. Every group's `suppliers.find(s => s.id === sid)` then fails, so every supplier renders as 'ללא ספק' even though supplier_debts.supplier_id is populated correctly. SuppliersClient.tsx and DebtsClient.tsx don't hit this: SuppliersClient uses `select('*')` (no error on a missing column, just omits it) and DebtsClient's suppliers query never asks for opening_balance at all.
fix: Code-side: added an else-if in loadAll() to showToast the suppRes.error message instead of failing silently, so a missing-column/RLS error surfaces immediately next time instead of masquerading as 'ללא ספק' on every row. Real fix requires applying migration 066_supplier_opening_balance.sql to the live Supabase DB (ALTER TABLE suppliers ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS opening_balance numeric(10,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0) — could not run this myself, no DB credentials in this environment. RESOLVED 2026-07-13: user ran migrations 066-070 directly in the Supabase SQL Editor (wrapped in BEGIN/COMMIT), confirmed 'Success. No rows returned' — opening_balance column now exists on the live DB, closing the actual root cause.

error_message: צ'קים שהגיע מועד הפירעון שלהם עדיין מוצגים עם כפתור '✓ שולם' ידני, ובפאנל ההתראות מוצגים כ'באיחור' — למרות שסוכם שצ'ק אמור להיסמן כשולם אוטומטית ברגע שמגיע התאריך
root_cause: scheduled_payments.is_paid was a fully manual field — nothing in the codebase (no trigger, cron, or client-side logic) ever flipped it to true when due_date arrived. statusInfo() in ScheduledPaymentsModal and the AlertsPanel query both derive the overdue/paid state purely from is_paid + due_date, so an unpaid-but-overdue check correctly (given the missing logic) rendered as late with a manual pay button.
fix: Added lib/utils/autoMarkOverdueChecks.ts (autoMarkOverdueChecksPaid) which, for payment_method='check' rows with is_paid=false and due_date<=today, race-guards an update to is_paid=true+paid_date then inserts the matching expenses row and backfills expense_id — mirroring the manual markPaid() side effects. Called from ProfileContext.load() (session-level, admin-gated) plus defensively re-run in ScheduledPaymentsModal.fetch() and AlertsPanel.load() for sessions left open across midnight. Transfers remain manual (not in scope).

error_message: בבדיקות קניה (טופס בדיקה לפני קניה) הדפסת הטופס הפסיקה לסמן ✓/✗ בעמודות תקין/לא תקין — הטבלה יוצאת ריקה לגמרי
root_cause: ok/fail status for a system was only ever resolved inside goNext() (the '›' next-arrow / 'לסיכום' button). Every other way of leaving a step — goPrev ('‹'), jumpTo (clicking a numbered pill), and the direct setStep(TOTAL)/setStep(0) calls on the '≡' summary pill and '← חזור' button — moved `step` without committing the item's status, so any system reviewed via those paths stayed at its initial '' status forever and printed as a fully blank row (no ✓, no ✗, no fault text) in printChecklist's makeRow().
fix: Extracted a shared commitCurrentStep() (resolves items[step].status from faults, skips 'na' items) and call it at the top of goNext, goPrev, and jumpTo. Rewired the two remaining direct setStep(TOTAL)/setStep(0) call sites to go through jumpTo(TOTAL)/jumpTo(0) instead so no navigation path skips the commit.

error_message: Vercel deploy 'Failed to compile' — ./components/supplier-tracking/SupplierTrackingClient.tsx:1285:24 Type error: Argument of type '(prev: Supplier[]) => QuickSupplier[]' is not assignable to parameter of type 'SetStateAction<Supplier[]>' — Property 'opening_balance' is missing in type 'QuickSupplier' but required in type 'Supplier'
root_cause: QuickAddSupplierModal.tsx's onCreated callback hands back a QuickSupplier (id/name/phone/contact_name only). SupplierTrackingClient's setSuppliers(prev => [...prev, s]...) pushed that raw QuickSupplier straight into Supplier[] state, but Supplier (components/supplier-tracking/SupplierTrackingClient.tsx:50-53, added alongside migration 066_supplier_opening_balance.sql) requires a non-optional opening_balance: number. This was a pure type-level gap (tsc wasn't runnable in earlier sessions — no node_modules — so it slipped past review) that only surfaced when Vercel ran `npm run build`'s TypeScript check.
fix: In the onCreated handler, spread the QuickSupplier and default opening_balance: 0 (matches the DB column's default for a brand-new supplier row with no invoices yet): setSuppliers(prev => [...prev, { ...s, opening_balance: 0 }]...)

error_message: יצירת סדרת צ'קים ליום 10 בחודש שומרת את הצ'קים ביום 9
root_cause: In the series-creation loop, `new Date(fDue + 'T00:00:00')` is parsed as LOCAL time, but the row was serialized with `dueDate.toISOString().slice(0,10)`, which converts to UTC before slicing. In Israel (UTC+2/+3), local midnight falls on the previous UTC calendar day, so every generated row (including the first, i=0) landed one day earlier than selected.
fix: Added a local-only date formatter `toLocalISODate(d)` (built from getFullYear/getMonth/getDate, no UTC conversion) and used it instead of `.toISOString().slice(0,10)` for the series due_date. Single-payment save path already wrote `fDue` directly (no Date round-trip) and was unaffected.

error_message: יצירת סדרת צ'קים מתוך מסך תשלום ספק לא משייכת אוטומטית לחוב שסומן ('שלם הכל' / סימון ידני נשכח)
root_cause: SupplierTrackingClient.submitPayment()'s check-method branch only forwarded paySupplierId into ScheduledPaymentsModal via schedInitialSupplierId — paySelectedIds/payAllocAmounts (the debts the user had just checked off) were discarded. ScheduledPaymentsModal's auto-opened add form always reset selectedDebtIds/debtAllocAmounts to empty whenever the supplier changed, so buildAllocations() returned empty and reconcileSupplierPayment was skipped unless the user manually re-selected debts inside the checks modal.
fix: Added initialSelectedDebtIds/initialDebtAllocAmounts props to ScheduledPaymentsModal, carried via a pendingInitialAllocRef consumed once inside the open-debts fetch (fetchOpenDebtsAndSeed), seeding the selection (filtered to debts still open) instead of resetting to empty. SupplierTrackingClient now passes paySelectedIds/payAllocAmounts through on the check-method redirect and clears the new state in the modal's onClose.

error_message: אחרי אישור יצירת סדרת צ'קים, או אחרי עריכת תשלום קיים, מודל הצ'קים 'נפתח מחדש' במקום להיסגר
root_cause: ScheduledPaymentsModal renders two sibling <Modal> instances (list + add/edit form) gated by the same parent `open` prop combined with a local formOpen flag. All three save-success branches (edit/series/new) only called setFormOpen(false), never the parent's onClose() — so with `open` still true, the LIST modal immediately reappeared right after a successful save, reading as 'the checks modal reopened.' Confirmed openEdit() (editing an existing payment) is only reachable from within that list modal, so the edit flow hit the identical mechanism.
fix: Added an onClose() call after setFormOpen(false) in all three save-success branches, so the whole modal closes on successful save instead of falling back to the list. Deliberate UX choice per user report — no 'stay on list for rapid multi-add' behavior preserved.

error_message: בכרטסת לקוח סימנתי תשלום, המסך חישב נכון אבל בסיכום הסופי (הדפסת כרטסת) יתרת החוב לא הופחתה
root_cause: In the printMode==='ledger' printed table, the running 'יתרה בפועל' column only accumulated raw charge/credit amounts (`running += item.amount` or `-item.amount`) and never subtracted `d.paid` — unlike `openingForReport` right above it, which already nets payments via `bal(d)`. So a payment recorded on a debt correctly reduced the on-screen totals (which use bal()) but the printed ledger's final running balance stayed at the full charge amount, only showing the payment separately in the 'שולם' footer figure.
fix: Subtract `Number(d.paid)` from `running` once per debt (on its last invoice-line item, for direction!=='credit') right after the existing charge/credit accumulation, so the in-range running balance stays consistent with openingForReport's bal()-based carry-in.

error_message: בסריקת ברקוד ברחבה (הוספת מוצר לרכב בטיפול): פריט שאזל מהמלאי מציג 'ברקוד לא נמצא' במקום הודעת מלאי, ופריט אחר שנוסף בסריקה לא הוריד מהמלאי של המוצרים
root_cause: Two separate defects found in the same yard scan flow: (1) neither WorkCardClient.handleBarcodeScan nor FreeSearchClient.handleBarcode checked the matched item's `stock` field — a matched-but-out-of-stock product/tire was either added silently with no warning, or (in FreeSearchClient) `item` was accessed without a null-check, crashing instead of showing a clear message. (2) Stock deduction only happens once, when a yard session is archived (app/api/yard/sessions/[id]/route.ts PATCH status='archived'), via `decrement_tire_qty`/`decrement_product_qty` RPCs (migration 053_yard_rpcs.sql). Those RPCs run `UPDATE tires/products SET quantity = ...` but the real column on both tables is `qty` (confirmed in supabase/migrations/003_all_tables.sql) — `quantity` doesn't exist, so the UPDATE errors every time. The rpc() call's `error` was never checked in route.ts, so the failure was completely silent and stock never actually decremented, on any session, ever.
fix: 1) Added an explicit `item.stock !== null && item.stock <= 0` check in both scan handlers, showing 'פריט אזל מהמלאי: <name>' and returning before adding. Also fixed FreeSearchClient.handleBarcode to null-check `item` (the API returns HTTP 200 with a null body on no-match, so `!res.ok` never actually catches a real 'not found'). 2) Added migration 071_fix_yard_decrement_qty_column.sql with CREATE OR REPLACE for both RPCs pointing at `qty` instead of `quantity`. 3) Added error logging (console.error) around both rpc() calls in route.ts so a future failure surfaces instead of vanishing silently, per the bug-005 Do-Not-Repeat convention.

error_message: כל הדפסת כרטסת (לקוח/ספק) יוצאת עם דף ריק נוסף
root_cause: Both ledger print flows use the 'hide everything via visibility:hidden, show #print-area via position:absolute' technique. `visibility:hidden` does not remove elements from layout flow, so AppShell's `<main>` (components/layout/AppShell.tsx) — which has an explicit `height: calc(100vh - var(--header-h))` + `overflowY:auto` — still occupies roughly one viewport-height's worth of empty box in the printed document, right alongside the absolutely-positioned print-area. The browser paginates for that leftover height, producing one extra blank page after the real content. (Header/Sidebar are `position:fixed` so they don't contribute — `main` was the only in-flow block with an explicit height.)
fix: Added `main { height: auto !important; overflow: visible !important; }` inside both components' existing `@media print` blocks, so the shell's fixed-height scroll container collapses to zero contribution during print instead of leaving a blank trailing page.

error_message: בסייד-בר, כשעורכים סידור/קטגוריות ושומרים, בפעם הבאה שנכנסים לעריכה זה מציג את הסידור המקורי (ברירת מחדל) ולא את העריכה האחרונה שנשמרה
root_cause: SidebarLayoutEditor's open-effect always seeded its local editing state (`sections`) from the `defaultSections` prop. Sidebar.tsx passed the hardcoded module-level `SECTIONS` constant as `defaultSections` — never the current `activeSections` state (which reflects the last save, loaded from localStorage/tenant ui_settings on mount). So `onSave` correctly persisted the edit and updated `activeSections` (the sidebar itself rendered correctly), but reopening the editor modal re-read from the static default every time, discarding the save from the picker's own point of view.
fix: Added a new `currentSections` prop to SidebarLayoutEditor, passed as `activeSections` from Sidebar.tsx. The open-effect now seeds `sections` from `currentSections` instead of `defaultSections`; `defaultSections` (the true SECTIONS constant) is kept only for the 'איפוס לברירת מחדל' reset button, which must still restore the real defaults, not the last save.
